meta.title: Lamma — Intimate Kuwaiti Gatherings
meta.description: A Kuwaiti platform for curated intimate gatherings. We treat gatherings as conversation, not transaction.

about.manifesto: We do not sell events. We open doors. Every gathering is a small circle around a table or a rug, hosted by someone who knows their guests' names. No tickets, no queues, no cameras. Only a question, tea, and slow time. We believe a city dies when its small gatherings die, and that every gathering is an act of resistance against transaction. We do not want to grow — we want to deepen.

intimate.title: Intimate, not scalable
intimate.desc: Every gathering has a ceiling. When the room is full, the door closes. Quality precedes quantity.

editorial.title: Editorial, not promotional
editorial.desc: We curate gatherings the way an editor curates essays — with taste and a question, not numbers.

slow.title: Slow, not rushed
slow.desc: A gathering needs time. Not an hour, but an evening. Not a programme, but a flow.

prayerAware.title: Prayer-aware
prayerAware.desc: It begins after the call to prayer, not before. Time bends to the city's rhythm, not the calendar's.

about.storyBody: Lamma began in the winter of 2025, when five people sat on a rug in an old diwaniya and decided that gatherings in their city had become commerce, not conversation. They wanted small gatherings, hosted by someone who knew their guests, attended by those who cared, not those who paid. They wrote their first manifesto in a notebook, then opened the first five gatherings. Today we are more, but still small — and we know each other's names.
whyNow: The city is growing fast, and small gatherings are disappearing just as fast.
Commercial events have become a substitute for meeting, not a bridge to it.
After two pandemics, we know that physical presence is irreplaceable.
New voices need a patient audience, not a large one.
In an age of speed, slowness is resistance — and Lamma is slow.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: What newcomers ask before their first gathering.
faq.a1: Lamma is a Kuwaiti platform for curated intimate gatherings. Every gathering is a small circle (8–50 people) hosted by someone who knows their guests, around a specific editorial topic.
faq.q2: How do I join a gathering?
faq.a2: Pick a gathering from the listings, read its description, then submit an application via the application form. The host reviews applications and accepts those they find suitable.
faq.a3: Some gatherings are free, others are paid to cover materials or venue. The price is clearly stated on each gathering's page.
faq.q4: How large is a typical gathering?
faq.a4: Between 8 and 50 people, depending on the topic. We never exceed the ceiling — even if hundreds ask.
faq.a5: Yes, we recommend it. A gathering is a chance to meet new faces. If you bring a friend, sit together but meet the others.
faq.q6: How long does the application take?
faq.a6: A short form: your motivation, and one or two questions from the host. About 5 minutes usually.
faq.a7: Usually within 3–7 days. You'll receive a message with an approval, decline, or waitlist.
faq.a8: Yes, from your dashboard. But please don't cancel after acceptance unless it's an emergency — the seat was held for you.
faq.q9: What if my application is declined?
faq.a9: Decline is not personal. The host picks those they find most suited to the topic. Try another gathering — there are many.
faq.q10: How do I become a host?
faq.a10: Register as a host from your account, then create your first gathering. Lamma will review it before publishing.
faq.q11: What is expected of a host?
faq.a11: To welcome guests by name, to respect prayer times, to write an honest description, and to review applications in good faith.
faq.q12: Do I get paid for hosting?
faq.a12: If your gathering is paid, most of the fee covers materials. Lamma takes a small commission to cover platform operations.
faq.a13: Yes. Specify this when creating the gathering, and guests will be asked to respect the privacy of the venue.
faq.a14: After your application is accepted, you'll be asked to pay electronically with a supported card. We don't accept cash at the venue.
faq.a15: Within 48 hours of payment, yes. After that, only up to 72 hours before the gathering, and only for an emergency.
faq.a16: Yes, an invoice is sent to your email after payment.
faq.q17: What is the code of conduct?
faq.a17: We observe conversational ethics: no interrupting, no escalation, no exchange of money or cards at the gathering. The gathering is for conversation, not commerce.
faq.q18: What happens if someone breaches the code?
faq.a18: The host may end the gathering. Lamma may ban the offender from future attendance.
faq.q19: Is Lamma suitable for all ages?
faq.a19: Some gatherings are family-friendly (like the Towers gathering), others are adults only. This is clearly stated on each gathering's page.
faq.a20: Via hello@lamma.kw or a direct message on Instagram @lamma.kw. We reply within 48 hours.
